content.full_text:
RAvI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
March24, 1997
Fred Dyzynske
City Councilmember
City ofPortola
35 Third Avenue
Post Office Box 1225
Portola, California 96112
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. G-97-116
Dear Mr. Dyzynske:
This letter is a response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the “Act”).’
Specifically, you have asked whether a fellow Portola city
councilmember should abstain from voting on projects being undertaken by the city. The
California Code of Regulations states that written advice can be solicited by “any person whose
duties under the Act are in question or by that person’s authorized representative.” (Regulation
18329(b)(1) and 18329(c)(1), copy enclosed.)
Your letter indicates that the city attorney has
advised a newly appointed councilmember that he should abstain from any vote on a current
water project being undertaken by the City ofPortola. However, nothing in your letter indicates
that you are acting as the representative ofthe newly appointed councilmember. Therefore, we
are unable to provide you with any advice on this matter.
For your information, I enclose a copy of the Commission’s “Guide to the Political
Reform Act of 1974.” The guide addresses the questions and concerns raised in your letter and
should be a helpful resource for you. I have also enclosed a copy ofregulation 18703 which sets
Government Code sections 81000
- 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 -
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660


File No. G-97-116
Page 2
forth the standards applicable to public officials who are disqualified from participating in
governmental decisions but who may participate ifthe “public generally” exception applies.
Should you have a specific prospective question relative to your conduct, please feel free to seek
written assistance in the future.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-
5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel
By: Deborah Maddux-Allison
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
SGC:DMA:ak
Enclosure

sections.question: Whether a fellow Portola city councilmember should abstain from voting on projects being undertaken by the city.
sections.conclusion: We are unable to provide you with any advice on this matter.
sections.question_synthetic: The requester asked whether a newly appointed city councilmember should abstain from voting on a water project undertaken by the City of Portola.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide advice because the requester was not acting as an authorized representative of the newly appointed councilmember, as required by Regulation 18329(b)(1) and 18329(c)(1).
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter where the FPPC refuses to answer because the requester lacks standing. The letter contains a clear question and clear conclusion, though the conclusion is negative (inability to advise rather than substantive legal guidance).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a fellow Portola city councilmember should abstain from voting on projects being undertaken by the city.

CONCLUSION: We are unable to provide you with any advice on this matter.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to provide written advice to Fred Dyzynske regarding whether a fellow councilmember should abstain from voting on city projects, finding he was not authorized to request advice on behalf of that councilmember. The Commission enclosed guidance materials and invited him to seek advice regarding his own prospective conduct.
